name: Selkirk Bannock (Scottish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a loaf tin.
2. Dissolve the sugar in the milk and stir in the yeast until well combined. Let sit in a warm place for 15 minutes.
3. In a bowl, sift together flour and salt. Add butter and rub into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
4. Make a well in the center of the mixture and pour in the yeast mixture. Mix vigorously until a soft dough forms.
5. Turn out onto a floured surface. Knead gently to form a ball. Transfer to a greased bowl, cover, and let rise for 30 minutes.
6. Gently work the sultanas into the dough with your hands, ensuring they are evenly distributed. Re-form the dough ball and let rise for another 30 minutes.
7. Carefully transfer the dough to the prepared tin. Let it rise for an additional 45 minutes.
8. Brush the top with beaten egg.
9. Bake in a pre-heated oven at 350°F (Mark 4) for 20 minutes, or until golden brown in color.
10. Turn onto a wire rack and allow to cool before serving sliced thinly and buttered.
